name: Weekly Git Branch Cleanup Report — Find Stale & Merged Branches

parameters.content:
## 🧹 Stale Branch Cleanup Reporter

### 👤 Who's it for
Engineering leads and DevOps teams drowning in old Git branches. Reports merged-but-not-deleted and inactive branches so you can reclaim repository clarity.

### ⚙️ What it does
1. Runs on a weekly schedule (or on-demand via webhook).
2. Reads your repo list from config.
3. Fetches all branches and recently merged PRs per repo.
4. Classifies branches as merged (PR merged, branch still alive) or stale (no commits for N days).
5. Skips protected branches (`main`, `master`, `develop`, `release/*`).
6. Posts a Slack report listing the top candidates.
7. In **report-only mode** (default), no branches are deleted.
8. If you enable auto-delete (`reportOnly: false`), stale and merged branches are removed with a safety cap.

### 🛠️ How to set up
• Attach **GitHub** (PAT with repo delete) and **Slack** credentials.
• Edit **Configuration**: repo list, stale age, protected branches.
• Start with `reportOnly: true` — only switch to auto-delete after reviewing reports.

🔑 **Credentials needed**
• GitHub PAT: `repo` (delete requires write)
• Slack Bot (`chat:write`)

🛡️ **Safety**
`reportOnly: true` by default → no deletions happen unless you opt in.

parameters.name: query
parameters.value: =query($owner: String!, $name: String!) { repository(owner: $owner, name: $name) { refs(refPrefix: "refs/heads/", first: 100) { nodes { name target { ... on Commit { committedDate } } } } } }
